message:
🐛 Fix GA4 anomalies: agent_token_failure trending + ksc_error spike (#11052)

Route auth failure emitters (emitAgentTokenFailure, emitWsAuthMissing,
emitSseAuthFailure, emitSessionRefreshFailure) through the existing
emitError() throttle system instead of calling send('ksc_error', ...)
directly. This subjects them to the per-category 60s throttle window
and per-page session budget that all other ksc_error events respect.

Additionally, add a session-level dedup flag in shared.ts so repeated
agent token fetch failures from polling hooks (every 30-60s) only
emit one GA4 event per page load, matching the pattern already used
by wsAuth.ts for ws_auth_missing.

Root cause: polling hooks calling agentFetch() every 30-60s would
re-attempt getAgentToken() on each failure (agentTokenPromise reset
to null), firing unthrottled ksc_error events — causing the 4→17→60
trend (#10996) and contributing to the 3.6× ksc_error spike (#11006).

Signed-off-by: ks-ci-bot <ks-ci-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ks-ci-bot <ks-ci-bot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
